title: Mid-America Apartment Communities, Inc. (MAA) CEO Eric Bolton on Q1 2022 Results - Earnings Call Transcript
text: Mid-America Apartment Communities, Inc. (NYSE:MAA ) Q1 2022 Results Conference Call April 28, 2022 10:00 AM ET Company Participants Eric Bolton - Chairman and CEO Al Campbell - Executive VP, CFO Robert DelPriore - Executive VP, CAO and General Counsel Tom Grimes - Executive VP, COO Brad Hill - Executive VP, Chief Investment Officer Tim Argo - Executive VP, Chief Strategy and Analysis Officer Andrew Schaeffer - Senior VP, Treasurer and Director of Capital Markets Conference Call Participants Neil Malkin - Capital One Brad Heffern - RBC Capital Markets Austin Wurschmidt - KeyBanc Nicholas Joseph - Citi Nick Yulico - Scotiabank John Kim - BMO Capital Markets Alexander Goldfarb - Piper Sandler Anthony Powell - Barclays Rob Stevenson - Janney Haendel St. Juste - Mizuho Chandni Luthra - Goldman Sachs Rich Anderson - SMBC Tayo Okusanya - Credit Suisse Operator Good morning, ladies and gentlemen, and welcome to today's MAA First Quarter 2022 Earnings Conference Call. [Operator Instructions] As a reminder, this conference call is being recorded today, April 28, 2022.

title: Mid-America Apartment: Steady As She Goes
text: The outlook for Apartment REITs remains bright, due to a host of secular tailwinds. Mid-America Apartments is the third-largest Apartment REIT in America by market cap.

title: Mid-America Apartment Communities: Great Core Holding And Inflation Hedge, At The Right Price
text: MAA's Sunbelt apartment portfolio is well-diversified and extraordinarily well-positioned for today's inflationary environment and beyond. The REIT is a wannabe core holding of mine, but it is difficult to determine a good buy price because the current situation is significantly different than the pre-COVID environment.

title: Mid-America Apartment (MAA) Beats Q1 FFO Estimates, Ups '22 View
text: Mid-America Apartment's (MAA) Q1 core FFO & revenues outpace estimates. Rental revenues and physical occupancy witness growth.

title: Mid-America Apartment Communities (MAA) Q1 FFO and Revenues Beat Estimates
text: Mid-America Apartment Communities (MAA) delivered FFO and revenue surprises of 2.60% and 0.40%, respectively, for the quarter ended March 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: What's in Store for Mid-America Apartment (MAA) in Q1 Earnings?
text: Mid-America Apartment's (MAA) Q1 results are likely to reflect the improving demand for its apartment units, though the elevated supply might act as a deterrent.

title: 3 Real Estate Stocks to Buy if You're a Housing Bull
text: Housing shortages and record selling pricing in the U.S. fuel demand and boost cash flow for real estate stocks. The post 3 Real Estate Stocks to Buy if You're a Housing Bull appeared first on InvestorPlace.

title: I'm Not Buying Digital Real Estate -- I'm Doing This Instead

title: Are You Looking for a High-Growth Dividend Stock? Mid-America Apartment Communities (MAA) Could Be a Great Choice
text: Dividends are one of the best benefits to being a shareholder, but finding a great dividend stock is no easy task. Does Mid-America Apartment Communities (MAA) have what it takes?

title: This Real Estate Stock Is Predicting Another Year of Strong Growth
text: Mid-America Apartments was in the right places at the right time for the pandemic, but management still expects more good things ahead.

title: MAA Announces Date of First Quarter 2022 Earnings Release, Conference Call
text: GERMANTOWN, Tenn. , April 6, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- MAA (NYSE: MAA) announced today that the Company expects to release its first quarter 2022 results on Wednesday, April 27, 2022 after market close and will hold a conference call on Thursday, April 28, 2022 at 9:00 a.m.

text: Apartment REITs did exceptionally well in 2021.
